antiwork/gumroad#4.body:
# Background

Currently, the help pages are static HTML files in the /public/help/ directory. PR #29857 attempted to add the main navigation sidebar to these pages using JavaScript injection when users are logged in, but feedback indicated that the proper solution would be to move these pages into the main app structure.

# Requirements

- Move help pages from /public/help/ into Rails views
- Implement a dedicated HelpController to serve these pages
- Properly integrate the main navigation sidebar for logged-in users
- Maintain existing help page functionality and styling
- Ensure all category and article pages are migrated properly

# Technical Details

## Current Architecture

* Help pages are static HTML files in /public/help/
* They already make a call to /users/session_info to check authentication status
* Main navigation is implemented as a React component in app/javascript/components/server-components/Nav.tsx

## Proposed Architecture

1. Create a new HelpController with actions for index, category, and article views
2. Create corresponding view templates in app/views/help/
3. Migrate content from static HTML files to these view templates
4. Include the main navigation component in the layout for logged-in users
5. Update routes.rb to handle all help page URLs
6. Implement proper redirects from old paths for SEO

# References

* PR #29857 attempted to add navigation via JavaScript: https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/pull/29857
* Main navigation component: app/javascript/components/server-components/Nav.tsx
* Session info endpoint: app/controllers/users_controller.rb

# Implementation Notes

This is not a trivial change as it involves moving many static files into the application and setting up proper routing. It should be planned carefully to maintain all existing functionality while adding the requested main navigation sidebar.
antiwork/gumroad#4.title: Move help pages into main app structure for proper navigation integration

antiwork/gumroad#13.body: ### What Add a new “Churn” tab to the Analytics page, visible only to creators with subscription products. The chart is filterable by daily/monthly, product, and date range as in sales. Show **Churn rate**, **Last period churn rate**, **Revenue lost**, and **Churned users** in the highlights to give creators a quick snapshot of how their subscriptions are doing both in terms of retention and revenue. New feature flag: `churn_analytics_enabled` **Desktop:** **Mobile:** **Post-deployment** * [x] Enable the feature to select sellers * [x] Small Bets / dvassallo (?) * [x] Enable the feature for everybody * [ ] Tweet and email blast **After complete rollout** * [x] Remove `CreatorAnalytics::Churn::DemoData` (introduced by #2037) #### Churn Calculation: ``` Churn rate for a period = (Number of cancelled subscriptions in the period / (Number of active subscriptions at the beginning of the period + Number of new subscriptions added in the period)) x 100 ``` Where _period_ can be a day or a month or a custom date range depending on the selected filters (please see designs above). In highlights, "Churn rate" is current period's (day/month/custom date range) churn rate, "Last period churn rate" is last period's (day/month/custom date range) churn rate, "Revenue lost" is the sum of monthly recurring revenue for the subscriptions churned in the current period, and "Churned users" is the number of churned subscriptions in the current period. Some reference docs on churn rate calculation are [here](https://support.stripe.com/questions/calculating-subscriber-churn-rate-in-billing), [here](https://stripe.com/in/resources/more/customer-churn-rates-101#how-to-calculate-churn-rate), [here](https://stripe.com/in/resources/more/how-to-calculate-churn-rates), and [here](https://stripe.com/in/resources/more/monthly-churn-101). ### Why To give creators better insight into where their revenue is coming from and how quickly it's shrinking/growing.

## Description
Currently, the `value` field in the metadata sent to Helper only includes sales from the last 28 days plus recent purchases. We need to update this to include lifetime sales to improve support for all creators who have contributed to Gumroad's lifetime revenue.

## Implementation Details
- Update the `value` calculation in `app/services/helper_user_info_service.rb` around line 35-36
- Current implementation: `value: user.sales_cents_total(after: 28.days.ago) + purchases_cents_total(after: 28.days.ago)`
- Keep the current 28-day calculations and add lifetime sales to it
- The `user.sales_cents_total` method (without the `after` parameter) can be used to get lifetime sales

## Tests to Update
- Update tests in `spec/services/helper_user_info_service_spec.rb` to verify lifetime sales are included
- Update controller tests in `spec/controllers/api/internal/helper/users_controller_spec.rb` that verify the metadata value

## Why
This change will improve support for all creators who have contributed to Gumroad's lifetime revenue by providing more comprehensive information to Helper.

antiwork/gumroad#164.body:
# Bug Report — “Home” Navigation Link Never Shows Active State

While navigating **from About back to Home**, I kept clicking **Home** because it never highlighted, so I assumed the click hadn’t worked. Every other nav item shows an active (black) state, but Home does not.

---

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Visit <https://gumroad.com> on desktop
2. Click **About** → the “About” link turns black (active)  
3. Click **Home** to return to the landing page

## Expected Result  
The “Home” link (or the logo) adopts the same black active-state styling used by “About,” “Pricing,” etc., indicating the current page.

## Actual Result  
The “Home” link remains light-gray—identical to inactive links—so users receive no visual confirmation and may assume their click failed.

---

## Root Cause

| File | Line(s) | Details |
|------|---------|---------|
| `app/javascript/components/Home/Nav.tsx` | 29-33 | Faulty comparison sets `isCurrent` |
| | 161-167 | “Home” link uses `Routes.root_url()` (absolute URL) |
| `app/javascript/components/useOriginalLocation.ts` | 5-10 | Hook returns **full absolute URL** (`window.location.href`) |
| `config/routes.rb` | root declaration | `js-routes` exports `root_url` as absolute URL |

### Faulty Logic

```ts
// Nav.tsx
const currentLocation = useOriginalLocation();                // e.g. "https://gumroad.com/"
const isCurrent = href === new URL(currentLocation).pathname; // pathname is "/"
...
<NavLink text="Home" href={Routes.root_url()} />              // "https://gumroad.com"
```

Because `"https://gumroad.com" !== "/"`, `isCurrent` is **always false** for Home, so it never receives the `active` styles or `aria-current="page"` attribute.  
All other links compare pathnames to pathnames (e.g., `"/about"`), so their active states work correctly.

---

## Suggested Fix

Compare pathnames on both sides (or use `Routes.root_path()`):

```ts
// Nav.tsx  (replace the faulty line)
const isCurrent =
  new URL(href, currentLocation).pathname === new URL(currentLocation).pathname;
```

This change gives **Home** the same `active` class and `aria-current="page"` attribute as the other links, restoring visual consistency and meeting WCAG 2.1 navigation-orientation guidance.
antiwork/gumroad#164.title: Bug Report — “Home” Navigation Link Never Shows Active State

# Only issue one license key for gifted purchases (to the giftee)

## Current Behavior
Currently, when a product with a license key is purchased as a gift, both the gift sender (gifter) and the gift receiver (giftee) may receive license keys. This creates confusion and potential licensing issues.

## Expected Behavior
Only the gift receiver (giftee) should receive the license key. The gift sender (gifter) should not receive a license key since they are not the intended user of the product.

## Technical Details
Based on the codebase:

1. The `Purchase` model has flags for both `is_gift_sender_purchase` and `is_gift_receiver_purchase`
2. The `linked_license` method in the `Purchase` model tries to get the license from either the current purchase or the giftee's purchase
3. The receipt presenter already has logic to not show license keys for gift sender purchases, but the issue appears to be that license keys are still being generated for both purchases

## Implementation Notes
The fix should ensure that license keys are only generated for the giftee's purchase record and not for the gifter's purchase record.

## Why This Matters
- Prevents confusion for customers
- Ensures proper license management
- Aligns with customer expectations (the person receiving the gift should be the one with the license)
antiwork/gumroad#193.title: Only issue one license key for gifted purchases (to the giftee)

## Overview
Currently, Gumroad applies a standard fee structure to all sellers:
- Direct sales: 10% + 50¢ Gumroad fee + payment processor fees
- Discover sales: 30% flat fee

We need to implement a feature that allows setting custom fee percentages for specific sellers.

## Business Need
Some sellers may qualify for custom fee rates based on:
- Sales volume
- Strategic partnerships
- Special promotions or campaigns
- Legacy agreements

Having the ability to set custom fees per seller would provide flexibility in our business relationships and potentially attract high-volume creators to the platform.

## Technical Requirements

### User Model Changes
- Add fields to store custom fee percentages:
  - `custom_direct_fee_percentage` (default: null)
  - `custom_discover_fee_percentage` (default: null)

### Purchase Model Changes
- Update fee calculation logic to check for custom fees before applying standard fees
- Ensure backward compatibility with existing purchases

### Admin Interface
- For now, we can have these set via `rails console`, no need for a UI.
- Include validation to ensure fees are within reasonable ranges
- Display within admin dashboard for user (coming back soon)

## Implementation Considerations
- Fee changes should only apply to new purchases, not retroactively
- Add appropriate test coverage for the new fee calculation logic
antiwork/gumroad#236.title: Implement Custom Fee Structure for Sellers

antiwork/gumroad#264.body:
Gumroad provides upsells that work for a handful of cases, but are fundamentally broken in others – to the point where I can't use them right now, because they would result in customers paying more than they should.

The problem:

- I sell Product A for $50.
- I sell Bundle B including Product A for $150.
- Both Product A and Bundle B have a discount code to save 50%. *The discount code is the same for both items.*
- If you add Product A to your basket using my discount code, you see the discount code and are asked to pay $25.
- But if I add an upsell to Bundle B, the user is immediately prompted to pay $150 – the discount appears in the list of coupons, but they don't get the discount applied.
- If you cancel payment for Bundle B then refresh the page, the discount *is* applied.

The correct behavior here is to apply the upsell (switch from Product A to Bundle B), then immediately reduce the price based on whatever discount code was applied previously. The whole upsell process should show the correct price, from start to finish, with the discount applied.

Thank you!
antiwork/gumroad#264.title: Upsells are broken when product + bundle share the same discount code

# Omani IBAN Support: Account Number Validation Blocks 23-Character IBANs

## Problem Summary

A Gumroad creator in Oman is unable to update their payout settings because the platform's account number validation rejects their valid 23-character IBAN. This has completely blocked their payouts since June 3rd, 2025.

## User Impact

- **User**: sahil.lavingia@gmail.com
- **Last Successful Payout**: June 3rd, 2025
- **Current Status**: Payouts completely blocked
- **Support Escalation**: User has already tried standard support channels including AI assistant

## Technical Root Cause

The `OmanBankAccount` model in `app/models/oman_bank_account.rb` has overly restrictive validation:

```ruby
ACCOUNT_NUMBER_FORMAT_REGEX = /^[0-9]{6,16}$/
```

This regex:
- Only accepts numeric digits (0-9)
- Limits length to 6-16 characters
- Rejects valid 23-character Omani IBANs which contain letters

## Bank Requirement Change

The user's bank in Oman now **requires** IBAN format for all incoming payments. The valid Omani IBAN is 23 characters long, but Gumroad's validation shows "invalid account number" error.

## Existing Solution Pattern

The codebase already supports IBAN validation for European accounts in `app/models/european_bank_account.rb`:

```ruby
def validate_account_number
  return if Ibandit::IBAN.new(account_number_decrypted).valid?
  errors.add :base, "The account number is invalid."
end
```

The `Ibandit` gem is already included in the Gemfile and supports IBAN validation.

## Proposed Solution

Update the `OmanBankAccount` model to support IBAN format:

1. Modify `ACCOUNT_NUMBER_FORMAT_REGEX` to accept 23-character IBANs
2. Consider using `Ibandit::IBAN` validation similar to European accounts
3. Ensure backward compatibility with existing numeric account numbers
4. Update any related frontend validation

## Priority

**High** - This is blocking a creator's ability to receive payments, directly impacting revenue.

## Additional Context

- Omani IBANs follow the format: OM + 2 check digits + 4 bank code + 16 account number = 23 characters total
- This affects international compliance as banks modernize their payment systems
- Similar issues may arise for other countries transitioning to mandatory IBAN usage

## Files to Review

- `app/models/oman_bank_account.rb` (primary fix location)
- `app/models/european_bank_account.rb` (reference implementation)
- Frontend validation components for Omani bank accounts
- Any payment processor integration code for Oman
antiwork/gumroad#489.title: Omani IBAN Support: Account Number Validation Blocks 23-Character IBANs

antiwork/gumroad#545.body:
# Improve payout error messaging to distinguish between Stripe and admin-initiated pauses

## Problem

Currently, when payouts are paused, users see a generic "paused by the admin" message that doesn't accurately reflect who actually paused the payouts. This is misleading because:

1. **Stripe often pauses payouts automatically** due to verification issues, compliance requirements, or other payment processor policies
2. **Gumroad admins rarely pause payouts manually** - when they do, they leave specific notes explaining why
3. **Users receive confusing messaging** that suggests Gumroad staff paused their payouts when it was actually Stripe

## Current Implementation

The payout error message is generated in `app/business/payments/payouts/payouts.rb` line 20:

```ruby
if user.payouts_paused?
  paused_by = user.payouts_paused_internally? ? "admin" : "creator"
  user.add_payout_note(content: "Payout on #{payout_date} was skipped because payouts on the account were paused by #{paused_by == 'admin' ? 'the admin' : 'you'}.") if add_comment
  return false
end
```

The issue is that `payouts_paused_internally` is automatically set to `true` by Stripe when they disable payouts (see `app/business/payments/merchant_registration/implementations/stripe/stripe_merchant_account_manager.rb` lines 751-752), making it appear as if the admin paused payouts.

## Context from Support Team

From Steve K in #gumroad-support:
> "If a payout is 'paused by the admin' that typically means Stripe has paused the payouts, not me. I will leave a note if I'm pausing someone's payouts, so that the next time I see the account, I'll know why I did that."

## Proposed Solution

Improve the messaging to distinguish between different types of payout pauses:

1. **Stripe-initiated pauses**: "Payouts are currently paused by our payment processor. Please check your Payment Settings for verification requirements."
2. **Admin-initiated pauses**: "Payouts have been paused by Gumroad support. [Include admin note if available]"
3. **User-initiated pauses**: "You have paused your payouts."

## Implementation Considerations

- Need to track the source of payout pauses (Stripe vs manual admin action)
- Consider adding a `pause_reason` or `paused_by_source` field to distinguish between different pause types
- Update the payout note generation logic to provide more specific messaging
- Ensure admin notes are displayed when available for manual pauses
- Update help documentation to reflect the new messaging

## Files to Review

- `app/business/payments/payouts/payouts.rb` - Main payout logic and message generation
- `app/business/payments/merchant_registration/implementations/stripe/stripe_merchant_account_manager.rb` - Stripe payout status updates
- `app/controllers/admin/users/payouts_controller.rb` - Admin payout controls
- `app/javascript/components/server-components/BalancePage.tsx` - Frontend display
- `app/views/help_center/articles/contents/_281-payout-delays.html.erb` - Help documentation

## Related

This issue was identified through support team feedback about confusing payout error messages that don't accurately reflect the source of payout pauses.
antiwork/gumroad#545.title: Improve payout error messaging to distinguish between Stripe and admin-initiated pauses

antiwork/gumroad#585.body:
# Public Payouts REST API (V2)

## Summary
Create a public REST API for payouts in `/api/v2/payouts` following existing API patterns, with read-only access to payout data.

## Background
Currently, Gumroad has comprehensive payout functionality via `Api::Internal::Helper::PayoutsController`, but no public API for external developers. The internal API includes payout history, balance information, manual payout creation, and CSV exports.

## Proposed Solution
Develop a public REST API following the existing `/api/v2/` patterns:

### Endpoints (Read-Only)
- **GET /api/v2/payouts** - List payouts with pagination
- **GET /api/v2/payouts/:id** - Get specific payout details

### Implementation Details
- **Controller**: `Api::V2::PayoutsController` extending `Api::V2::BaseController`
- **Authentication**: Doorkeeper OAuth with appropriate scopes (similar to sales API)
- **Response Format**: Standard `{ success: true, payouts: [...] }` pattern
- **Documentation**: Add to `config/initializers/api_v2_methods.rb` for auto-generated docs

### API Scope
Following existing patterns in `config/routes.rb`:
```ruby
resources :payouts, only: [:index, :show]
```

### Response Structure
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "payouts": [
    {
      "id": "payout_123",
      "amount": "150.00",
      "currency": "USD",
      "status": "completed",
      "created_at": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z",
      "processed_at": "2025-01-02T00:00:00Z",
      "payment_processor": "stripe"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Technical Implementation

### Leverage Existing Code
- Reuse logic from `Api::Internal::Helper::PayoutsController`
- Follow authentication patterns from `Api::V2::SalesController`
- Use existing payout models and services

### Security & Performance
- OAuth scope-based access control
- Rate limiting via existing middleware
- Proper data filtering (only user's own payouts)
- Pagination for large datasets

### Documentation
- Add to API methods configuration
- OpenAPI schema generation
- Developer portal integration

## Success Metrics
- API adoption by external developers
- Reduced support requests for payout data access
- Performance benchmarks matching other V2 endpoints

## Related Files
- `app/controllers/api/internal/helper/payouts_controller.rb` (existing internal API)
- `app/controllers/api/v2/base_controller.rb` (base controller pattern)
- `app/controllers/api/v2/sales_controller.rb` (similar API pattern)
- `config/routes.rb` (routing patterns)
- `config/initializers/api_v2_methods.rb` (API documentation)
antiwork/gumroad#585.title: Public Payouts API

antiwork/gumroad#950.body:
# What

Enhance the payouts API (`/v2/payouts`) to include upcoming/open payouts with real-time data, in addition to the currently available closed payouts.

Currently, the payouts API only returns past closed payouts, leaving creators approximately 2 weeks behind on their payout data. Creators need access to upcoming payout information that Gumroad already calculates in real-time (visible in the dashboard balance).

The enhancement should:
- Include upcoming payouts in the API response alongside closed payouts
- Add an `open` status flag to distinguish upcoming payouts from closed ones
- Provide real-time payout data instead of the current ~2 week delay

# Why

Creators currently face a painful workflow to get a holistic view of their payouts:
1. Download separate CSV files for each individual past payout
2. Manually concatenate multiple files to get complete payout history
3. Still miss upcoming payout data entirely

This enhancement would:
- Eliminate the need for manual CSV file concatenation
- Provide programmatic access to complete payout data (past + upcoming)
- Enable real-time financial planning and reporting for creators
- Match the real-time data already displayed in the Gumroad dashboard

The payouts API should be as comprehensive as the sales API, providing both historical and upcoming data through a single programmatic interface.
antiwork/gumroad#950.title: Enhance payouts API to include upcoming/open payouts with real-time data

### What

**PR1**

* [x] Update create purchase logic to save `max_refund_period_in_days` value on `PurchaseRefundPolicy` records
* [x] Create one-time script to update `max_refund_period_in_days` on older `PurchaseRefundPolicy` records
  * **Important!** Use `purchase_refund_policies.title` value to determine it, not `refund_policies.max_refund_period_in_days` (as it may have changed)

_Post-deployment_
* [x] `output = Onetime::SetMaxAllowedRefundPeriodForPurchaseRefundPolicies.new.process_with_logging `

**PR2**
* [x] Add validation for `max_refund_period_in_days` on `PurchaseRefundPolicy`
* [x] Update `PurchaseRefundPolicy#different_than_product_refund_policy?` to use `mas_refund_period_in_days`

### Why

Ref https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/pull/1016#discussion_r2304128937

> It's not great having the comparison done on the title, as it can change (the structure of it). A better way would be to have it done on [`max_refund_period_in_days`](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/blob/87afd34afd24908c79f38cf36afb157244c93d67/db/schema.rb#L1622), however, that [is not set for product-level refund policies, only for account-level refund policies](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/blob/87afd34afd24908c79f38cf36afb157244c93d67/app/services/purchase/create_service.rb#L46-L57) (which were [deprecated in April](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/issues/74)).
> 
> Also, `PurchaseRefundPolicy#max_refund_period_in_days` is [used](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/blob/87afd34afd24908c79f38cf36afb157244c93d67/app/modules/purchase/refundable.rb#L328-L332) for [auto-refunds](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/blob/87afd34afd24908c79f38cf36afb157244c93d67/app/controllers/api/internal/helper/purchases_controller.rb#L609-L611). So that not being set, makes me think that auto-refund feature ([introduced in March](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad-old/pull/29830), before we reverted account-level refund policies) is not actually working.

antiwork/gumroad#1125.body:
When setting up Gumroad locally (Node v20.17.0, npm v10), running `make local` 
fails during Webpack compilation with:

  /node_modules/tty doesn’t exist

This happens because Webpack 5 no longer polyfills Node core modules by default.

**Workaround:**
Adding a `resolve.fallback` entry in `webpack/common.js` for `tty` and other 
missing modules (like `fs`, `path`) allows Webpack to compile successfully.

**Suggestion:**
- Add this config to the repo’s webpack config, or
- Update setup docs to mention the workaround.

Environment:
- macOS Sequoia
- Node v20.17.0 (via nvm)
- npm v10.x
antiwork/gumroad#1125.title: Webpack build fails due to missing Node core modules (tty not found)

Today, any eBook published on Gumroad.com doesn't publicize its ISBN so that the ISBN is indexed and searchable on sites like GoodReads.com. 

ISBNs for eBooks can be bought from sites from freeISBN.com. For example, the Office 365 for IT Pros eBook has ISBN 979-8-89170-497-8. However, although the ISBN is included in the book content (PDF and EPUB) and the description of the book on Gumroad.com, the book cannot be found using the ISBN or title on any site that indexes books by ISBN. This puts Gumroad creators at a considerable disadvantage.

Gumroad should publish ISBNs for eBooks (when provided by creators) to make the ISBNs indexable and searchable.
antiwork/gumroad#1145.title: Gumroad doesn't support ISBNs

antiwork/gumroad#1178.body:
# Add Toggle Functionality for Upsells

## Problem
VIP users have requested the ability to toggle Upsells on and off. Currently, the only option is to delete them entirely, which removes access to historical data and analytics.

## Current Behavior
- Upsells can only be **deleted** (soft deleted via `deleted_at` timestamp)
- Once deleted, upsells disappear from the dashboard and their data becomes inaccessible
- Users lose visibility into past performance metrics when they want to temporarily disable an upsell

## Requested Feature
Add a toggle switch to **activate/deactivate** upsells while preserving all historical data:

- **Active upsells**: Show in checkout flow and dashboard (current behavior)
- **Inactive upsells**: Hidden from checkout but still visible in dashboard with analytics data
- **Deleted upsells**: Completely removed (existing behavior preserved)

## Benefits
- Sellers can temporarily disable underperforming upsells without losing data
- Easy to reactivate successful upsells from previous campaigns
- Better analytics continuity for business decision making
- Reduces accidental data loss from premature deletions

## Technical Notes
- Current system uses soft deletion (`deleted_at` field) 
- Would need new `active` boolean field to distinguish inactive vs deleted states
- Frontend toggle control needed in `UpsellsPage.tsx` drawer alongside Edit/Delete
- Backend API endpoint for toggle functionality
- Update checkout flow to respect active status

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add toggle switch in upsells dashboard
- [ ] Inactive upsells don't appear in checkout flow
- [ ] Inactive upsells remain visible in dashboard with "Inactive" status
- [ ] Analytics/statistics preserved for inactive upsells
- [ ] Existing delete functionality unchanged
antiwork/gumroad#1178.title: Add Toggle Functionality for Upsells

### What

Updates to a product’s price or installment configuration (like the number of installments) are impacting the remaining installments of existing purchases.

For example, [this product's price](https://sketchitbr.gumroad.com/l/carsketchitcourse) was changed from $147 to $197, which caused the installment amount to jump for customers who had already purchased.

In another report, the number of installments was changed from 3 to 2, which also retroactively affected ongoing installment schedules.

### Expected behavior
Once a customer starts an installment plan, the remaining installments should remain locked to the original purchase agreement (price and number of installments) and not be affected by later changes to the product setup.

### Context

CI is failing because of a series of flaky system tests related to the tipping feature, preventing auto-deployment as a consequence

### Problem
On the checkout form, users experience two issues with the tip input:

1. Whenever a user tries to enter a tip, the first keystroke causes the input box to lose focus

Both of these issues occur because `<TipSelector />` is conditionally renderered in multiple locations inside `PaymentForm`

antiwork/gumroad#1670.body:
###  What

- The public Payouts API (`/api/v2/payouts/:id`) should return all the same details as the exported payout CSV
- Add a new `transactions` key to the public payouts API response which would list all the balance affecting transactions in that payout period, same as the exported payout CSV:
  - `transactions` can be an array like `[type, date, purchase_id, item_name, taxes, shipping, sale_price, gumroad_fees, net_total]` where `type` will be Affiliate Credit, Affiliate Fees, Chargeback, Credit, Full Refund, Partial Refund, PayPal Connect Affiliate Fees, PayPal Payouts, PayPal Refund, Sale, Technical Adjustment etc. as listed in the exported payout CSV.

### Why

- To help with bookkeeping and bank account reconciliation
- The `sales` and `refunded_sales` records listed in the payouts API currently are mutable
antiwork/gumroad#1670.title: Public Payouts API should return the same details as the exported payout CSV

antiwork/gumroad#1777.body: ### What Add additional text on the receipt and customize the text of "Get Content" button. Context: Prior, we used to have a feature to add text to email receipts. This was removed when we shipped the content editor because the content editor allows this level of functionality. However, it is still very useful to have control over the email receipt, and the button content (e.g. “See your content” vs. “Join the community”) to maximize conversion and the legitimacy of the product. Make it clear that **the creator has added this content** to the receipt so that the buyer doesn’t get confused on what is part of every Gumroad receipt and what is part of the product. We are not considering multiple notification templates like Shopify–it would require hundreds of engineering hours. It’s a nice idea to investigate later: A form on the left that allows customizing the subject and body of the email receipt using a Liquid-like templating language. Example [v0](https://v0.app/chat/build-customizable-receipts-t53NLWpCpZW?b=v0-preview-b_OwvRinIwBeh&f=1&path=%2F): Gumroad workflow email edit/preview for inspiration: Current Gumroad product edit page: **Recommended PRs:** - [ ] Add receipt customization to main product edit page - [ ] Add new receipt tab with preview on right ### Why Requested by multiple major Gumroad creators. Also, customers may decide what product to use (Gumroad vs Paddle for example) based on whether this feature exists (e.g. asking ChatGPT - which digital product sales platforms have a custom receipt feature?).
antiwork/gumroad#1777.title: Customizable receipts

antiwork/gumroad#2699.body:
## Problem

There are two PayPal credential issues causing test failures:

### 1. Pages won't render (90% of failures)

When `PAYPAL_PARTNER_CLIENT_ID` is missing/null, the checkout page crashes before it even loads:

```
Error: Expected root.paypal_client_id to be a string, got null
```

This breaks every test that visits a checkout or subscription page. Tests fail with "Unable to find css h4" because there's nothing on the page.

**Fix**: Add a dummy value like `PAYPAL_PARTNER_CLIENT_ID=test-paypal-partner-client-id`. The page will render, tests can interact with it.

### 2. PayPal iframe test (1 test)

One test checks that PayPal's SDK renders an iframe:

```
expect(page).to have_selector("iframe[title=PayPal]")
```

This iframe only appears when PayPal's JavaScript SDK loads with a **real** client ID. Dummy credentials fix the page rendering but won't make this specific test pass.

## Options for the iframe test

**A) Skip when using dummy credentials**
```ruby
skip "Requires real PayPal credentials" if PAYPAL_PARTNER_CLIENT_ID&.start_with?("test-")
```

**B) Test that we pass the right props instead of checking the iframe**

Verify Gumroad passes `paypal_client_id` correctly. Trust that PayPal's SDK will do its job.

**C) Use real PayPal sandbox credentials**

Like we do with Stripe. Full coverage but requires external account setup.

**D) Remove the iframe assertion**

The test is verifying PayPal's SDK works, not our code.

## Required changes

Add to `.env.test`:
```bash
PAYPAL_PARTNER_CLIENT_ID=test-paypal-partner-client-id
```

Note: `PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID`, `PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET`, and `PAYPAL_PARTNER_CLIENT_SECRET` are not required. The `PayPal::SDK.configure` call tolerates nil values at boot, and VCR cassettes handle actual API calls with placeholder filtering.

Adding the dummy value will fix the page rendering issue and unblock ~90% of failing tests. For the iframe test, we need to decide which option (A-D) makes the most sense before this can be fully resolved.
antiwork/gumroad#2699.title: Fix PayPal checkout test to run without secrets

antiwork/gumroad#3028.body:
Follow up of https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/issues/856

> [!NOTE]
> This is a parent issue. PRs should refer to the corresponding sub-issue.

### Context

We are gradually providing Gumroad with a full Single Page App experience by converting our React on Rails / ERB templates to Inertia templates.

### General instructions

- Add your Inertia template to `app/javascript/pages`. The template name should match the controller endpoint, e.g. for the given`EmailsController#scheduled` controller action, the template should be `Emails/Scheduled`
- If the original template (typically in React on Rails) calls internal API endpoints to load subsequent data, use [partial reloads](https://inertia-rails.dev/guide/partial-reloads#partial-reloads) instead and delete those endpoints and the JS functions calling them
- If the original template uses client-side routes (typically via `react-router-dom`), convert those routes to actual server routes and serve them with a dedicated Inertia page. Common code across Inertia pages should be factored into a layout.
- Forms should be converted so they leverage [`useForm`](https://inertia-rails.dev/guide/forms#form-helper), including for client-side and server-side validations, loading state (`processing`), data manipulation and transformation, and submissions.

### Additional tips
- Instance variables in controllers should become local if they are no longer used in views
- Prefer `router.get` over `router.visit` if you intend to send a GET request
- Prefer `router.reload` over `router.get` to the same URL
- Success flash messages should be set server-side in the controllers since they are intercepted automatically in the Inertia layouts. You should not set them on the `onSuccess` callback of an Inertia visit
antiwork/gumroad#3028.title: Migrate the remaining pages from React on Rails to Inertia

antiwork/gumroad#3036.body:
## Description

Sellers can price products at the currency minimum, but those minimums don't always cover Gumroad's fees. When fees exceed the product price, the purchase is blocked — buyers see a confusing "product is not correctly set up" error and Gumroad earns $0.

This also affects products where purchasing power parity discounts or currency conversion reduce the effective price below the fee threshold.

## Impact

- **50 purchases** failed in the last 3 months due to fees exceeding the product price.
- **16,603 successful purchases** last month had less than 20 cents of margin between price and fees – any small rate fluctuation could tip them into failure.
- Typical failure: a $0.50 effective price where fees total $0.55. The sale fails over 5 cents.
- Gumroad currently earns $0 on every one of these because the purchase is blocked entirely.
- Raising currency minimums was considered but would break **333K+ existing products**.

## Proposed fix

**Cap fees at checkout instead of blocking the sale.**

When calculated fees (Gumroad's cut + Stripe processing) equal or exceed the product price, cap fees so the seller nets at least 1 cent. Gumroad takes a reduced cut instead of earning nothing.

Example on a $0.50 transaction (currently everyone gets $0):
- Stripe processing: ~$0.31
- Gumroad: ~$0.18
- Creator: $0.01

This only applies when the price covers Stripe's processing cost. When the price is too low even for that, the purchase still fails – but with a clearer error message for both buyer and seller.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] When Gumroad fees + affiliate commission >= product price, fees are capped so the seller nets at least 1 cent
- [ ] When even zeroing Gumroad fees isn't enough (e.g. affiliate commission alone >= price), the purchase still fails
- [ ] Buyer error message on remaining failures is updated from "product is not correctly set up" to something about the price being too low to process
- [ ] Seller notification email gives clearer, actionable advice
- [ ] No change in behavior for purchases where fees are below the price
- [ ] Tests cover: fee capping, remaining failure case, no-change case
antiwork/gumroad#3036.title: Currency price minimums don't account for fees

antiwork/gumroad#3453.body:
# What?
We need a way to throttle upload speed in system specs so we can assert mid-upload UX (e.g. “Files are still uploading…” tooltips, disabled  buttons, “Some images are still uploading, please wait…” when switching tabs).

Previously, file-upload specs ran against AWS S3, and the extra latency made it easy to hit those intermediate states. After moving uploads to MinIO, they often finish before the test can assert them. 

As a result, specs that rely on mid-upload behavior were temporarily disabled and we lost coverage for that UX. Throttling in the test gives us back a reliable way to hit and assert those states.

# Ref

### Description

When uploading a cover image on the product edit page, there is a noticeable delay (around 4–5 seconds) before the loading indicator appears. During this time, there is no visible feedback to the user, which may create confusion about whether the upload has started.

---

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to **Product → Edit** page  
2. Upload a cover image (tested with 300KB–10MB images)  
3. Observe the time before the loading spinner appears  
---

### Current Behavior

- After selecting a file, there is a 4–5 second delay.  
- The loading spinner appears only after this delay.  
- Users may think the upload did not trigger.  
---

### Expected Behavior

- The loading indicator should appear immediately after file selection.  
- Users should receive instant visual feedback that the upload process has started.

antiwork/gumroad#3669.body:
## What

Gumroad collects sales tax in 60+ countries but only produces a monthly summary report for US states. The accounting team needs a single monthly CSV that summarizes **GMV, order count, and tax collected** across **all** tax-collecting jurisdictions – grouped by country, with state/province breakdown where applicable.

## Why

The existing `CreateUsStatesSalesSummaryReportJob` was built exclusively for US states – it uses zip code lookups, US subdivision mapping, and TaxJar transaction pushing. There's no equivalent summary for international jurisdictions. Individual per-country reports exist (India monthly, Canada monthly, EU quarterly VAT) but they each have different formats, run on different schedules, and serve different purposes. There's no single view of tax collection across all jurisdictions.

## Proposed solution

Create a new `CreateGlobalSalesTaxSummaryReportJob` that generates a single monthly CSV covering all countries where Gumroad collects tax.

**Report format** (confirmed by accounting):

| Column | Description |
|---|---|
| Country | Country name (Column A per accounting's request) |
| State/Province | State/province code where applicable, blank otherwise |
| GMV | Total transaction value in USD, net of refunds |
| Number of orders | Count of successful, non-refunded, non-chargedback purchases |
| Sales tax collected | Total `gumroad_tax_cents` in USD, net of refunds |

**Grouping rules:**
- **US**: Break down by state (use existing zip code / GeoIP logic from the US summary job)
- **Canada**: Break down by province (use existing logic from `CreateCanadaMonthlySalesReportJob`)
- **India**: Break down by state (use existing `ip_state` logic from `CreateIndiaSalesReportJob`)
- **All other countries**: Country-level only (single row per country)

**Implementation steps:**

1. **Query purchases with tax collected** for the previous month: filter on `gumroad_tax_cents > 0`, successful, non-refunded, non-chargedback, with a Stripe transaction ID. This naturally captures all jurisdictions without needing to enumerate them. Note: this scopes GMV and order counts to **purchases where tax was actually collected**, not all purchases in tax-collecting jurisdictions. This is intentional – the report is a tax collection summary, not a full GMV report.

2. **Determine country from purchase data**: normalize the `country` field (handle variants like "South Korea" vs "Korea, Republic of", "Czechia" vs "Czech Republic"). Fall back to `ip_country` when `country` is nil, consistent with existing report jobs.

3. **Determine state/province for US, CA, IN** – reuse the existing detection patterns: zip code / GeoIP lookup for US, `state` field for Canada (the existing Canada report filters on `purchase.state` matching known CA subdivisions), `ip_state` for India.

4. **Aggregate** GMV (net of refunds), order count, and tax collected per country+state group.

5. **Output CSV** to S3 and **email it to @steveo-es and `salestax@gumroad.com`** – matching how existing monthly financial reports are delivered. The job runs automatically on the 1st of each month for the prior calendar month.

6. **Wire into `GenerateFinancialReportsForPreviousMonthJob`** so it runs automatically on the 1st of each month alongside existing reports.

**What this does NOT do:**
- Push transactions to TaxJar (TaxJar is US/CA-specific; keep that in the existing US job)
- Replace existing per-country reports (India, Canada, VAT) – those serve different purposes (transaction-level detail, VAT rate breakdowns, TaxJar compliance)
- Backfill past months (not needed per accounting)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] New job generates a CSV with columns: Country, State/Province, GMV, Number of orders, Sales tax collected
- [ ] US purchases are broken down by state
- [ ] Canada purchases are broken down by province
- [ ] India purchases are broken down by state
- [ ] All other countries appear as a single row per country
- [ ] All amounts are in USD
- [ ] Country names are normalized (no duplicate rows for "South Korea" / "Korea, Republic of", "Czechia" / "Czech Republic", etc.)
- [ ] CSV is uploaded to S3 and emailed to @steveo-es and `salestax@gumroad.com`
- [ ] Job runs automatically on the 1st of each month via `GenerateFinancialReportsForPreviousMonthJob`
- [ ] GMV is net of refunds and credits
- [ ] Existing US summary report and other per-country reports continue to work unchanged

## Edge cases

- **Country name inconsistencies**: The `country` field has variants ("Czech Republic" vs "Czechia", "Korea, Republic of" vs "South Korea"). Normalize to a canonical name – ideally map through ISO country codes rather than relying on raw strings.
- **Null country**: Some purchases have `country = NULL`. Fall back to `ip_country` as existing jobs do. If both are null, group under an "Unknown" row so nothing is silently dropped.
- **Large result set performance**: With ~294k purchases/month having tax, the query needs to be efficient. Consider using `find_each` or batching rather than loading all purchases at once. The existing US job loads purchase IDs first, then iterates – a similar pattern works here.
- **State/province detection failures**: For US/CA/IN, some purchases may not resolve to a state (bad zip, missing IP). Include an empty state/province value and still count them under the country total.
antiwork/gumroad#3669.title: Expand monthly sales tax summary report to cover all tax-collecting countries

antiwork/gumroad#3729.body:
## Summary

Remove legacy fee paths and constants from the codebase. All accounts should be on the current **10% flat fee + Stripe processing** model. The old pre-migration fee tiers and branching logic are dead weight.

## What to remove/simplify

### Dead constants
- `GUMROAD_FEE_PER_THOUSAND = 85` — old 8.5% rate, only used by `gumroad_fee_percentage_for_non_migrated_account`, should never apply anymore
- `GUMROAD_NON_PRO_FEE_PERCENTAGE = 60` — old 6% rate for "migrated" accounts, also obsolete
- Both `gumroad_fee_percentage_for_non_migrated_account` and `gumroad_fee_percentage_for_migrated_account` methods can go

### Simplify `calculate_gumroad_fee_per_thousand`
Current logic has three branches:
1. `flat_fee_applicable?` → 10% flat fee (the current model)
2. `seller.tier_pricing_enabled?` → old tier-based pricing
3. `else` → non-migrated vs migrated account branching

Branch 1 is the only one that should remain. Branches 2 and 3 should be removed. All accounts charge using their own Stripe account now, so the `charged_using_gumroad_merchant_account?` branching in fee calculation can likely be simplified too.

### Recurring subscriptions on old fee rates
Old subscriptions that predate the flat fee (`flat_fee_applicable?` returns false via `subscription.flat_fee_applicable?`) are still being charged at legacy rates. We should update these to the current 10% model.

### Keep
- `GUMROAD_FLAT_FEE_PER_THOUSAND = 100` (10%) — this is the standard rate
- `custom_fee_per_thousand` on User and Purchase — this is the manual per-creator discount override, still needed
- `GUMROAD_DISCOVER_*` constants — separate concern (Discover fees), out of scope
- `PROCESSOR_FEE_PER_THOUSAND` / `PROCESSOR_FIXED_FEE_CENTS` — Stripe's actual costs, still needed

## Open questions (for planning meeting)

- How many active subscriptions are still on legacy fee rates? (query subscriptions where `flat_fee_applicable?` is false)
- Do we migrate old subscriptions in one shot or gradually?
- Any creators still on `tier_pricing_enabled?` — can we just flip them to flat fee?
- Should we notify creators whose fee rate will change on recurring charges?

## Goal

`calculate_gumroad_fee_per_thousand` should simplify down to roughly:

```ruby
def calculate_gumroad_fee_per_thousand
  calculate_custom_fee_per_thousand
  (custom_fee_per_thousand.presence || GUMROAD_FLAT_FEE_PER_THOUSAND) + PROCESSOR_FEE_PER_THOUSAND
end
```
antiwork/gumroad#3729.title: Clean up legacy fee constants — standardize on 10% flat fee

## Issue
On screens with limited vertical height, the Sales filter dropdown extends off-screen. It lacks internal vertical scrolling, making the bottom inputs inaccessible.

## Steps to reproduce
- Navigate to the Sales page.
- Open the page on a device with limited vertical height (e.g., Nest Hub) OR shrink your desktop browser window vertically.
- Click the filter icon to open the filter menu.
- Attempt to scroll down to view the bottom of the filter menu.
- Observe that scrolling the page does not allow you to reach the bottom of the filter container, leaving the lower inputs completely inaccessible.

## Expected Behaviour
The filter container should have internal scrolling, so all options are accessible regardless of screen size.

## What actually happens
The filter dropdown content overflows the bottom of the screen and gets cut off. The user cannot scroll to see or interact with the hidden inputs.

antiwork/gumroad#3764.body:
When the Inertia migration is over (https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/issues/3763 and #3028), React on Rails will no longer be used and therefore should be removed

> [!NOTE]
> React on Rails is currently used in the ERB application layout to render common components like sidebars and flash messages.     This means a full migration of the app to Inertia is necessary before we can remove this dependency.

## What

4 out of the last 10 failed main branch CI builds have actual test failures (the other 6 fail only due to the "Claude Code Review" job, not tests). The flaky tests are blocking the automated production deployment pipeline.

### Updated flaky test analysis (Feb 25, 2026)

Based on the last 10 failed CI builds on main, **4 builds had test failures**:

| Failure Rate | Test | Description | Builds |
|---|---|---|---|
| **4/4 (100%)** | `spec/sidekiq/collect_unclaimed_balances_of_inactive_stripe_accounts_job_spec.rb:147` | does not attempt to collect balance if the Stripe account is considered active based on creation date | [1](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22403514953) [2](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22403495470) [3](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22402326609) [4](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22390567363) |
| **4/4 (100%)** | `spec/sidekiq/collect_unclaimed_balances_of_inactive_stripe_accounts_job_spec.rb:160` | does not attempt to collect balance if the Stripe account is considered active based on last payout date | [1](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22403514953) [2](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22403495470) [3](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22402326609) [4](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22390567363) |
| **4/4 (100%)** | `spec/sidekiq/collect_unclaimed_balances_of_inactive_stripe_accounts_job_spec.rb:173` | does not attempt to collect balance if the Stripe account is considered active based on last charge date | [1](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22403514953) [2](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22403495470) [3](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22402326609) [4](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22390567363) |
| **1/4 (25%)** | `spec/requests/settings/payments_spec.rb:1855` | AE business allows the creator to enter their business vat number | [1](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22403514953) |
| **1/4 (25%)** | `spec/requests/purchases/product/taxes_spec.rb:226` | US sales tax calculates and charges sales tax when WA customer makes purchase of a physical product | [1](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22402326609) |
| **1/4 (25%)** | `spec/requests/discover/discover_spec.rb:413` | displays thumbnail in preview if available | [1](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22390567363) |

Additionally, [one build](https://github.com/antiwork/gumroad/actions/runs/22402326609) had a mass infrastructure failure (56/77 failures in Slow 40), indicating CI runner instability.

### Root cause patterns

1. **Unstubbed Stripe API calls (NEW — #1 priority)** — The `collect_unclaimed_balances_of_inactive_stripe_accounts_job_spec.rb` tests make real `Stripe::Payout.list`, `Stripe::Charge.list`, and `Stripe::Balance.retrieve` calls that hit WebMock blocks, failing 100% of the time. These tests need proper Stripe API stubs.
2. **Capybara session cleanup issues** — stack traces frequently show `Capybara.reset_sessions!` errors, suggesting browser state leaks between tests.
3. **Infrastructure-level flakiness** — some runs had 50+ failures across unrelated files in a single run, pointing to CI runner resource constraints or Selenium/Chrome instability.
4. **Timing sensitivity in heavy specs** — the payments spec and discover spec are affected by implicit waits and async UI interactions.

Flaky tests make the main branch CI red, which blocks auto production deployment.

antiwork/gumroad#3814.body:
## What

If a Stripe webhook is missed, a seller's payout gets stuck in "Processing" forever. Their money is locked and we don't know unless they contact support. We already have a weekly sync job that catches this for PayPal payouts. Stripe payouts don't have one.

**In prod today:** 34 payouts are confirmed stuck (past Stripe's expected arrival date, real amounts). The oldest for over a year. Separately, 45 abandoned $0 records are stuck in "creating" and should be cleaned up.

- [ ] Add a scheduled job that checks Stripe for the actual status of overdue payouts and updates our records to match
- [ ] Only check payouts past their expected arrival date (skip ones legitimately in transit)
- [ ] Fail and unlock balances for payments stuck in "creating" for more than 48 hours (these never reached Stripe)
- [ ] Send the same deposit/failure emails that the normal webhook flow sends
- [ ] Run the job once manually to clean up the 34 long-stuck payouts
- [ ] Existing PayPal sync is unchanged

## Why

Stripe payouts depend entirely on incoming webhooks to move from "processing" to "completed" or "failed." Webhooks can be missed (network issues, deployment timing, retries exhausted). PayPal has the same problem, which is why we have the sync job for it.

## Open questions

- **Frequency**: Weekly (like PayPal) or daily? Daily catches problems faster but makes more Stripe API calls. In practice, only overdue payouts are checked, so the volume should be low.
- **Backfill strategy**: The 34 stuck payouts include some where the internal USD transfer may need to be reversed. Should we handle these in the automated job or review them manually first?
antiwork/gumroad#3814.title: Add automatic sync for stuck Stripe payouts

antiwork/gumroad#3837.body:
### Problem

The payments settings presenter sends `minimum_payout_amount_cents` (the effective minimum) to the frontend as `payout_threshold_cents` instead of the user's actual stored preference:

```ruby
# app/presenters/settings_presenter.rb:220
payout_threshold_cents: seller.minimum_payout_amount_cents,
```

`minimum_payout_amount_cents` is `max(payout_threshold_cents, minimum_payout_threshold_cents)` – it includes the country's cross-border minimum converted from local currency via exchange rate. The frontend initializes the form with this value and always sends it back on submission. The controller saves it directly.

This creates a ratchet effect: the country minimum (which fluctuates with exchange rates) gets "baked" into the user's stored `payout_threshold_cents` at peak rates and never comes back down, even when exchange rates drop or the user intended to keep the default $10 minimum.

### Reproduction

1. Be a seller in a country with a cross-border minimum > $10 (e.g. Argentina, Colombia, South Korea, Paraguay)
2. Visit Settings → Payments
3. Change any field (e.g. update bank account) and save
4. The country's exchange-rate-dependent minimum is now persisted as your `payout_threshold_cents`
5. If the exchange rate drops, your threshold stays at the old higher value

### Impact

Any seller in a cross-border payout country who submits the payments form has their threshold silently inflated. This may explain payout skips where users report a minimum they didn't set (e.g. antiwork/gumroad-private#166).

### Fix

Line 220 should send the user's stored preference, not the effective minimum:

```ruby
payout_threshold_cents: seller.payout_threshold_cents,
```

The frontend already receives `minimum_payout_threshold_cents` separately (line 221) and uses it for validation.
antiwork/gumroad#3837.title: Payout threshold gets silently overwritten on every settings save

antiwork/gumroad#3868.body:
### What

The "Town/Cho-me (Kana)" field on `/settings/payments` accepts fully Latin text (e.g., a building name like "Sunshine 301"). Stripe rejects this with `Invalid string: must provide a Kana variation` — but the error doesn't indicate which field is wrong, so sellers have no way to fix it themselves.

- [ ] Validate that `street_address_kana` and `business_street_address_kana` contain at least one katakana character (backend and frontend)
- [ ] Keep `building_number_kana` lenient — digits-only values like "5-3-4" are valid there

The `KANA_ADDRESS_REGEX` allows Latin characters (intended for building numbers), but the same regex is used for street address kana fields where Latin-only strings don't make sense.

### Why

Sellers see a confusing Stripe error with no indication of which field to fix, leading to support escalations. Ref: antiwork/gumroad-private#81

antiwork/gumroad#3871.body:
### What

On pages that conditionally render the `Search` popover component (Discounts, Followers), clearing the search input causes the search icon to briefly disappear and reappear.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e600c2db-d6f5-41ea-9c3e-c4e310cb39ca)

The guard conditions use a combination of data length and search query truthiness:

- **Discounts**: `offerCodes.length > 0 || searchQuery`
- **Followers**: `followers.length > 0 || searchQuery.length > 0`

When the user clears the input, `searchQuery` becomes `""` (falsy) immediately via `setState`, but the data hasn't reloaded yet. If the current (filtered) results are empty, both sides of the condition are false — the `Search` component unmounts (icon disappears). Once the reload completes with unfiltered data, the condition becomes true again and `Search` remounts (icon reappears).

We should replace the guard with a latching ref that preserves the original condition logic but, once true, never flips back to false. This keeps `Search` mounted through the reload while still hiding it on initial empty states (no data, no query).

### Why

The flicker is a visual glitch that affects any page using the `Search` component with conditional rendering. It's most noticeable when a search returns zero results and the user clears the input to go back to the full list.
antiwork/gumroad#3871.title: Search icon flickers when clearing search input

antiwork/gumroad#3882.body:
## What

Migrate the product edit page (`/products/:id/edit`) from React on Rails to Inertia. Same UX, same tabs, same save behavior – just a different page mount.

The approach:
- Add paired public-beta routes at `/products/:id/edit_new` and `/products/:id/edit_new/*other`
- Add a new `edit_new` action that reuses the same product-fetch/authorization/presenter setup as `edit`, but renders via Inertia
- Old `/edit` stays live during beta — both routes accessible to all creators
- Keep React Router for client-side tabs, keep `ProductEditContext`, keep global save
- Once parity is confirmed, swap `/edit` to use Inertia and clean up the old ERB/pack artifacts

Non-goals for this issue: no autosave, no redesign, no new save API flow.

The core change is mounting the same product-edit React UI through an Inertia page (`render inertia:`) on `edit_new`, while keeping the existing ERB + React on Rails mount for `/edit` during beta.

### CI validation

The scaffold PR validated full parity by running all 265+ existing edit system tests against `/edit_new`. Results:

- **1 real failure**: the products index page links to `/edit` (server-rendered). This is expected. The route swap hasn't happened yet and is the sole remaining work.
- **All other failures**: pre-existing flaky tests unrelated to our changes.

This means no incremental tab-by-tab test porting is needed. The migration is essentially complete in the scaffold PR.

### Task breakdown

**PR 1 — Scaffold** (adds `/edit_new` route with Inertia mount) — ready to merge
- [x] Add `edit_new` base + wildcard routes and `edit_new` controller action with `render inertia:`
- [x] Add Inertia page component in `app/javascript/pages/ProductEdit/`
- [x] Handle Dropbox JS loading (moved to React component or Inertia layout)
- [x] Update hardcoded edit paths in `ProductEditPage.tsx` and `ProductEdit/Layout.tsx` so both `/edit` and `/edit_new` work during beta
- [x] CI confirmed: all 265+ existing edit system tests pass against `/edit_new`

**PR 2 — Route swap and cleanup**
- [ ] Swap `edit_new` → `edit` as default route (update route definitions and helpers)
- [ ] Remove old ERB template (`links/edit.html.erb`)
- [ ] Remove `product_edit` pack entry
- [ ] Remove React on Rails registration for ProductEditPage (`packs/product_edit.ts`, SSR registry)

## Why

The product edit page is the last major page still on React on Rails. Migrating it to Inertia:
- Removes the last major page-level React on Rails usage (Nav, Alert, and CodeSnippet still use it)
- Aligns with the rest of the app (products index, bundle edit, etc. already on Inertia)
- Establishes the foundation for the product edit redesign (#1837)

The public beta at `edit_new` avoids feature flags, lets any creator test the new version, and provided a clear parity target. CI results from the scaffold PR confirmed that the existing test suite covers full parity. No new tests need to be written and no tab-by-tab migration is required. The remaining work is a single cleanup PR to swap the routes and remove the old ERB/pack artifacts.

Related: #3030, #1837, #3762
antiwork/gumroad#3882.title: Migrate product edit page from React on Rails to Inertia

antiwork/gumroad#3928.body:
## What

When an expired subscriber tries to re-subscribe through the product checkout page, the purchase fails with "The price just changed! Refresh the page for the updated price." Refreshing does not resolve it.

This affects any product where:
1. The product has an offer code applied to subscriptions
2. The offer code percentage was changed after subscribers originally purchased
3. Expired subscribers attempt to restart via the checkout page (not the manage page)

The checkout page computes the price using the **current** offer code percentage, but the backend computes using the **cached** percentage from when the subscriber originally purchased. These never match, so `validate_perceived_prices_match` always rejects the request.

A secondary issue: `RestartAtCheckoutService#updater_service_params` does not pass `quantity`, so `UpdaterService#same_quantity?` compares `original_purchase.quantity` against `nil` and always returns `false`. This forces `update_current_plan!` to run even for same-plan restarts, which triggers the discount mismatch path unnecessarily.

**Tasks:**
- [ ] Fix perceived price mismatch in the checkout restart flow
- [ ] Pass `quantity` in `RestartAtCheckoutService#updater_service_params`

## Why

The `RestartAtCheckoutService` bridges two contexts that compute prices differently:

- **Checkout frontend**: uses the product's current offer code (fetched live)
- **`UpdaterService` → `update_current_plan!` → `build_purchase`**: carries over the cached `purchase_offer_code_discount` from the original subscription purchase

When the offer code percentage hasn't changed, these happen to agree. Once the seller updates the percentage, every expired subscriber with the old cached value is permanently blocked from restarting through checkout — no user action (refresh, different browser, different device) can resolve it.

The manage page restart path is unaffected because it uses the frozen `current_subscription_price_cents` consistently on both frontend and backend.

The missing `quantity` param is a latent issue — it forces `update_current_plan!` on every checkout restart even when the plan isn't changing, which broadens the surface area for price mismatches.
antiwork/gumroad#3928.title: Expired subscribers cannot restart through checkout when offer code percentage has changed

antiwork/gumroad#3930.body:
## What

The UTM links pagination test (`spec/requests/analytics/utm_links_spec.rb:112` — "paginates the UTM links") is the most consistently flaky test on main, failing in 4+ separate CI runs recently.

The page was migrated to Inertia in PR #2604, which introduced two competing `router.reload` calls in `app/javascript/pages/UtmLinks/Index.tsx`:

1. **Pagination click** (line ~123): `router.reload({ data: { page: newPage } })`
2. **Stats fetch** (line ~101): a debounced (500ms) `router.reload` triggered by a `useEffect` whenever `utmLinks` changes

When the test clicks "Next", page 2 loads, then the stats `useEffect` fires and triggers a second reload. This causes a brief page flicker during Capybara assertions, leading to intermittent failures on `have_table_row` and `have_current_path` checks.

### Tasks

- [x] Fix the React component to avoid firing stats fetches during pagination (e.g., cancel the debounced fetch when pagination is in progress, or fetch stats server-side as part of the pagination response)
- [x] Alternatively/additionally, reorder test assertions so the page number button state is checked first as a synchronization point before asserting table content

## Why

This test fails in ~30% of CI runs on main, causing unnecessary re-runs and slowing down the entire team. The fix is small and well-understood.
antiwork/gumroad#3930.title: Fix flaky UTM links pagination test (router.reload race condition)

antiwork/gumroad#3931.body:
## What

Tax-related checkout tests (`taxes_spec.rb`, `shipping_spec.rb`) are the biggest source of CI flakiness by volume, with failures across 30+ test blocks on lines like 3221, 4040, 4089, 2507, 3478, 226, 83, 13.

The dominant pattern: tests assert exact tax/total amounts inside `check_out` blocks **before** clicking "Pay". Tax amounts are computed asynchronously after form fill (address, zip, country), so the assertion often runs before the async response arrives.

PRs #3822 and #3823 already fixed this for WA and Canada tests by removing pre-submit UI assertions and relying on post-purchase database assertions instead. The same pattern still exists in ~30 other test blocks.

### Tasks

- [ ] Remove pre-submit tax/total UI assertions from all remaining `check_out` blocks in `taxes_spec.rb` (follow the pattern from #3822 and #3823)
- [ ] Remove pre-submit UI assertions from `shipping_spec.rb` `check_out` blocks
- [ ] Add `force_vcr_on: true` to the `"sales tax", shipping: true` describe block in `taxes_spec.rb` to fix the VCR/JS conflict where `setup_js` turns off VCR but the shipping hook turns it back on with WebMock still allowing net connections

## Why

These flaky tests cause CI failures on nearly every run on main. The fix is mechanical (remove assertions, following existing precedent) and the post-purchase database assertions already verify tax correctness. The `taxes_spec.rb` file is 4362 lines and accounts for the majority of CI reruns.
antiwork/gumroad#3931.title: Remove pre-submit tax UI assertions from checkout tests

antiwork/gumroad#3932.body:
## What

Shipping checkout tests (`spec/requests/purchases/product/shipping/shipping_spec.rb` and related specs like `shipping_to_virtual_countries_spec.rb`, `shipping_offer_codes_spec.rb`) fail intermittently in CI.

Beyond the pre-submit assertion issue (tracked in a separate issue), these tests have additional flakiness sources:

1. **Stale VCR cassettes**: The file hasn't been touched since PR #1235 (2025). Cassettes may not match current API responses from EasyPost or tax endpoints, causing VCR to fail or let requests escape to the network.

2. **Currency conversion sensitivity**: Tests use GBP pricing with USD display. Expected amounts like `US$153.24` depend on the exchange rate at the time the cassette was recorded. Any drift breaks exact assertions.

3. **EasyPost address verification branching**: Tests with `should_verify_address: true` hit a branching flow with two 5-second waits (`has_text?(..., wait: 5)`). If EasyPost returns an unexpected response, the test takes the wrong branch or adds unpredictable delays.

### Tasks

- [ ] Re-record VCR cassettes for all shipping scenarios
- [ ] Pin currency conversion rates in tests (mock the rate or use USD-priced products)
- [ ] Add `:mock_easypost` tag to tests that don't specifically need real EasyPost responses
- [ ] Consider splitting the shipping tests into smaller, focused files

## Why

Shipping tests contribute to CI instability. The fixes are independent from the tax assertion cleanup and address environmental drift that will keep causing issues even after pre-submit assertions are removed.
antiwork/gumroad#3932.title: Fix flaky shipping spec tests (stale VCR cassettes + currency sensitivity)

antiwork/gumroad#4010.body:
## What

When creating an upsell with "Replace the version selected with another version of the same product", the version selection UI has two visual regressions:

1. **Version names render as plain text** — The "Version selected" column should display version names inside bordered input boxes, but they appear as unstyled text with no border, background, or padding.
2. **Arrow icon not vertically centered** — The arrow icon between the "Version selected" and "Version to offer" columns is top-aligned instead of vertically centered with the input rows.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Navigate to Checkout > Upsells
2. Click "New Upsell"
3. Select "Replace the version selected with another version of the same product"
4. Select a product with multiple versions

## Why

Regression introduced in #3891 (Migrate `_forms.scss` to Tailwind: Part 4). That PR added a `readOnly` variant to `InputGroup` that applies `bg-inherit border-none px-0`, stripping the bordered appearance. The upsell version grid uses `<InputGroup readOnly>` for version names, which now renders as plain text. The old `_forms.scss` styles also provided implicit vertical alignment that was lost in the migration.
antiwork/gumroad#4010.title: Fix broken "Version selected" UI in upsell creation form

antiwork/gumroad#4037.body:
## What
TikTok Pixel can fire some events today, but TikTok's Event Builder cannot reliably detect or interact with it when the pixel is injected through creator snippets inside a sandboxed iframe.

We should support TikTok Pixel as a first-class analytics integration, similar to Google Analytics and Facebook Pixel, so the pixel can be loaded in the main page document instead of through the sandboxed snippets path.

That would allow:
- TikTok's Event Builder to access page elements
- creators to configure TikTok Pixel using a pixel ID instead of custom script injection
- Gumroad to manage the standard TikTok event wiring directly

## Why
The sandboxed snippet path is the core reason TikTok's Event Builder cannot work reliably. This creates support load and limits the usefulness of TikTok Pixel for creators who want to map and validate events using TikTok's own tooling.

Treating TikTok Pixel as a first-class integration matches how other analytics providers are handled and avoids the iframe isolation problem.
antiwork/gumroad#4037.title: Support TikTok Pixel Event Builder by making TikTok Pixel a first-class analytics integration

antiwork/gumroad-mobile#97.body:
## Why

We are likely to make breaking changes to the API while building out the app. We should have a way to force users onto the latest app version so that they don't get a broken experience in an old version that expects different API responses.

## How

The usual way to do this seems to be to create an API endpoint which returns the minimum supported version. 

* We'll need to consider expo-updates as well, so we can create an API that returns something like `{ "minimumVersion": "2026.03.01", "minimumUpdateCreatedAt": "2026-03-12" }`
* Both these can be stored in Redis, and set manually in console when required (since Google Play / App Store / Expo don't provide an easy API to automate setting them)
* The API should be in `api/internal` rather than `api/mobile` since we'll deprecate the mobile endpoints after removing the old app. For similar reasons, we can require the new `account` Doorkeeper scope instead of the `mobile_api` one
* In the app, we can request this endpoint on start and check the version against `Constants.expoConfig?.version` and the update timestamp against `Updates.createdAt` (if present)
antiwork/gumroad-mobile#97.title: Support forcing users to update to a specific version
